**Guardrails**
- Favor straightforward, minimal implementations first and add complexity only when it is requested or clearly required.
- Keep changes tightly scoped to the requested outcome.
- Refer to `openspec/AGENTS.md` (located inside the `openspec/` directoryвЂ”run `ls openspec` or `openspec update` if you don't see it) if you need additional OpenSpec conventions or clarifications.
- Identify any vague or ambiguous details and ask the necessary follow-up questions before editing files.

**Steps**
1. Review `openspec/project.md`, run `openspec list` and `openspec list --specs`, and inspect related code or docs (e.g., via `rg`/`ls`) to ground the proposal in current behaviour; note any gaps that require clarification.
2. Choose a unique verb-led `change-id` and scaffold `proposal.md`, `tasks.md`, and `design.md` (when needed) under `openspec/changes/<id>/`.
3. Map the change into concrete capabilities or requirements, breaking multi-scope efforts into distinct spec deltas with clear relationships and sequencing.
4. Capture architectural reasoning in `design.md` when the solution spans multiple systems, introduces new patterns, or demands trade-off discussion before committing to specs.
5. Draft spec deltas in `changes/<id>/specs/<capability>/spec.md` (one folder per capability) using `## ADDED|MODIFIED|REMOVED Requirements` with at least one `#### Scenario:` per requirement and cross-reference related capabilities when relevant.
6. Draft `tasks.md` as an ordered list of small, verifiable work items that deliver user-visible progress, include validation (tests, tooling), and highlight dependencies or parallelizable work.
7. Validate with `openspec validate <id> --strict` and resolve every issue before sharing the proposal.

**Reference**
- Use `openspec show <id> --json --deltas-only` or `openspec show <spec> --type spec` to inspect details when validation fails.
- Search existing requirements with `rg -n "Requirement:|Scenario:" openspec/specs` before writing new ones.
- Explore the codebase with `rg <keyword>`, `ls`, or direct file reads so proposals align with current implementation realities.
<!-- OPENSPEC:END -->

## Complete Pattern: How `uv` Packages Are Installed

Based on my thorough search through your chezmoi dotfiles repository, here's the complete pattern for how `uv` packages flow from definition to installation:

### 1. Package Definition in `packages.yaml`

**File**: `/Users/craig/.local/share/chezmoi/home/.chezmoidata/packages.yaml` (lines 138-142)

```yaml
# In the "ai" tag section:
ai:
  taps: [...]
  brews: [...]
  casks: [...]
  uv:
  - "basic-memory"
  - "claude-monitor"
  - "git+https://github.com/cearley/claude-extend@latest"
  - "git+https://github.com/cearley/zsh-llm-suggestions"
```

**Key characteristics**:
- `uv` is a top-level key within any tag category (core, dev, ai, work, etc.)
- Supports three specification formats:
  - Simple package names: `"basic-memory"`
  - Git repositories: `"git+https://github.com/org/repo@latest"`
  - Version-pinned: `"package-name==1.0.0"`
- Currently only used in the `ai` tag; also works in other tags

### 2. Bun Packages Already Exist!

I found bun packages are **already defined** in your packages.yaml:

**File**: `/Users/craig/.local/share/chezmoi/home/.chezmoidata/packages.yaml` (lines 127-128)

```yaml
# In the "ai" tag section:
ai:
  bun:
  - 'ralph-tui'
```

However, **there is NO installation script for bun packages yet** - this is where the gap is.

### 3. UV Package Installation Script

**File**: `/Users/craig/.local/share/chezmoi/home/.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_before_darwin-25-install-tools.sh.tmpl`

```bash
{{ if eq .chezmoi.os "darwin" -}}
#!/bin/bash

source "{{ .chezmoi.sourceDir -}}/scripts/shared-utils.sh"

if ! require_tools uv; then
    exit 1
fi

print_message "info" "Installing tools using uv..."

{{- $categories := prepend .tag_choices "core" }}
{{- range $category := $categories }}
  {{- $categoryData := index $.packages.darwin $category }}
  {{- if or (eq $category "core") (has $category $.tags) }}
    {{- if and (hasKey $categoryData "uv") $categoryData.uv }}
# Install {{ $category }} tools
{{ range $categoryData.uv -}}
uv tool install {{ . }}
{{ end -}}
    {{- end }}
  {{- end }}
{{- end }}

{{ end -}}
```

**How it works**:
1. Runs only on macOS (`eq .chezmoi.os "darwin"`)
2. Sources shared utilities for consistent messaging
3. Requires `uv` to be already installed (position 30)
4. Uses Go template logic to:
   - Prepend "core" to tag choices
   - Iterate over each tag category
   - Check if category is "core" OR selected in user tags
   - Extract `uv` key from that category's data
   - Execute `uv tool install <package>` for each

**Key naming convention**: `run_onchange_before_darwin-25-install-tools.sh.tmpl`
- `run_onchange`: Re-runs when script or `packages.yaml` changes
- `before`: Runs before user scripts
- `darwin-25`: macOS platform, position 25
- Position 25 is specifically for "UV tool installation"

### 4. Complete Installation Sequence

```
Position 20: Install SDKMAN (if dev tag) в†ђ run_once_before
Position 23: Install Homebrew packages   в†ђ run_onchange_before
Position 24: Install SDKs via SDKMAN     в†ђ run_onchange_before
Position 25: Install UV tools            в†ђ run_onchange_before  <- bun should go here
Position 26: Install machine-specific Brewfiles в†ђ run_onchange_before
Position 30: Install UV itself           в†ђ run_once_before
```

### 5. Related Infrastructure: SDKMAN Pattern (for reference)

For comparison, here's how SDKMAN follows a similar pattern:

**Manager Installation** (position 20): `run_once_before_darwin-20-install-sdkman.sh.tmpl`
- Installs SDKMAN only once
- Conditional: `if and (eq .chezmoi.os "darwin") (has "dev" .tags)`

**Package Installation** (position 24): `run_onchange_before_darwin-24-install-sdks.sh.tmpl`
- Iterates through packages.yaml like UV script
- Sources init script if needed
- Executes `sdk install <package> <version>`

### 6. Package Management Spec Reference

From `/Users/craig/.local/share/chezmoi/openspec/specs/package-management/spec.md` (lines 287-314):

The spec defines exact requirements for UV tool management:
- Tools are defined in `packages.yaml` under tag categories with `uv` key
- Installation script runs at position 25 with `run_onchange_before` frequency
- Re-runs when script content or `packages.yaml` changes
- Idempotent: `uv tool install` handles already-installed packages
- Uses same tag-based selection as Homebrew (core always, others conditional)

## Summary: Pattern for Creating Bun Global Packages

To implement bun packages similarly, you would need:

1. **Data already exists**: Bun packages defined in `packages.yaml` under `ai` tag вњ…
2. **Missing**: A bun installation script at position ~27 or 28
   - Name: `run_onchange_before_darwin-27-install-bun-packages.sh.tmpl`
   - Similar structure to the UV tools script
   - Execute `bun add --global <package>` for each bun package

3. **Script requirements**:
   - Source shared utilities
   - Require `bun` to exist
   - Iterate through categories/tags
   - Execute installation command
   - Template-conditional on macOS

    11в†’
    12в†’if command_exists uv; then
    13в†’    print_message "info" "uv is already installed. Updating..."
    14в†’    uv self update
    15в†’    exit 0
    16в†’fi
    17в†’
    18в†’print_message "info" "Installing uv (Python package manager) using standalone installer from astral.sh..."
    19в†’
    20в†’# Install uv using the official installation script from astral.sh
    21в†’curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh
    22в†’
    23в†’print_message "success" "uv installed successfully!"
    24в†’print_message "info" "You may need to restart your terminal or source your shell profile"
    25в†’
    26в†’{{ end -}}
```
</details></tool-use>

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./openspec/specs/package-management/spec.md`

```text
     1в†’# Package Management System
     2в†’
     3в†’## Purpose
     4в†’The package management system provides a unified approach for installing packages across different ecosystems, all defined in a single `packages.yaml` file:
     5в†’- **Homebrew**: System packages, applications, and CLI tools (essential tag-based + machine-specific)
     6в†’- **UV Tools**: Python-based CLI tools and utilities (tag-based, within `packages.yaml`)
     7в†’- **SDKMAN SDKs**: JVM ecosystem SDKs and build tools (tag-based, within `packages.yaml`, requires `dev` tag)
     8в†’
     9в†’## Requirements
    10в†’### Requirement: Central Package Definition
    11в†’All packages SHALL be defined in `home/.chezmoidata/packages.yaml` organized by platform and tag.
    12в†’
    13в†’#### Scenario: Platform-based package organization
    14в†’- **WHEN** packages are defined for the darwin platform
    15в†’- **THEN** the YAML structure SHALL include `packages.darwin` with nested tag categories
    16в†’
    17в†’#### Scenario: Tag-based package categories
    18в†’- **WHEN** packages are categorized by tags (core, dev, ai, work, personal, datascience, mobile)
    19в†’- **THEN** each tag SHALL contain separate lists for `taps`, `brews`, `casks`, `mas`, `sdkman`, and `uv` packages
    20в†’
    21в†’### Requirement: Tag-Based Package Selection
    22в†’Package installation SHALL be controlled by user-selected tags during chezmoi initialization.
    23в†’
    24в†’#### Scenario: Core packages always installed
    25в†’- **WHEN** chezmoi applies configuration
    26в†’- **THEN** packages in the `core` tag SHALL be installed regardless of other selected tags
    27в†’
    28в†’#### Scenario: Optional tag packages
    29в†’- **WHEN** a user selects the `dev` tag
    30в†’- **THEN** only packages in `core` and `dev` tags SHALL be installed
    31в†’- **AND** packages in unselected tags SHALL be skipped
    32в†’
    33в†’#### Scenario: Multiple tag selection
    34в†’- **WHEN** a user selects tags `core,dev,ai,personal`
    35в†’- **THEN** packages from all four tag categories SHALL be installed
    36в†’
    37в†’### Requirement: Homebrew Package Types
    38в†’The system SHALL support all Homebrew package types: taps, brews (formulae), casks, and Mac App Store apps.
    39в†’
    40в†’#### Scenario: Homebrew tap installation
    41в†’- **WHEN** a tap is defined in `packages.yaml` under the `taps` key
    42в†’- **THEN** the tap SHALL be added via `brew tap` before installing packages from that tap
    43в†’
    44в†’#### Scenario: Formula installation
    45в†’- **WHEN** a package is listed under `brews`
    46в†’- **THEN** it SHALL be installed via `brew install <package>`
    47в†’
    48в†’#### Scenario: Cask installation
    49в†’- **WHEN** an application is listed under `casks`
    50в†’- **THEN** it SHALL be installed via `brew install --cask <package>`
    51в†’
    52в†’#### Scenario: Mac App Store installation
    53в†’- **WHEN** an app is listed under `mas` with format `"App Name", id: 123456`
    54в†’- **THEN** it SHALL be installed via `mas install 123456`
    55в†’
    56в†’### Requirement: Machine-Specific Brewfiles
    57в†’Additional packages SHALL be defined in machine-specific Brewfiles located in the `brewfiles/` directory.
    58в†’
    59в†’#### Scenario: Machine-specific Brewfile location
    60в†’- **WHEN** the machine configuration specifies `brewfile: mbp-brewfile`
    61в†’- **THEN** the system SHALL use `brewfiles/mbp-brewfile` for additional packages
    62в†’
    63в†’#### Scenario: Brewfile symlinking
    64в†’- **WHEN** chezmoi applies configuration
    65в†’- **THEN** the machine-specific Brewfile SHALL be symlinked to `~/Brewfile`
    66в†’- **AND** SHALL be accessible for manual `brew bundle` operations
    67в†’
    68в†’### Requirement: User Confirmation for Additional Packages
    69в†’Machine-specific Brewfile packages SHALL require explicit user confirmation before installation.
    70в†’
    71в†’#### Scenario: Confirmation prompt
    72в†’- **WHEN** a machine-specific Brewfile contains additional packages
    73в†’- **THEN** the script SHALL prompt the user for confirmation before running `brew bundle`
    74в†’
    75в†’#### Scenario: Confirmation denied
    76в†’- **WHEN** the user declines the Brewfile installation prompt
    77в†’- **THEN** the script SHALL skip Brewfile installation
    78в†’- **AND** SHALL continue with other setup tasks
    79в†’
    80в†’#### Scenario: Confirmation accepted
    81в†’- **WHEN** the user confirms the Brewfile installation prompt
    82в†’- **THEN** the script SHALL execute `brew bundle --file ~/Brewfile`
    83в†’
    84в†’### Requirement: Idempotent Package Installation
    85в†’Package installation SHALL be idempotent and safe to re-run.
    86в†’
    87в†’#### Scenario: Already installed package
    88в†’- **WHEN** a package is already installed
    89в†’- **THEN** `brew install` SHALL skip reinstallation
    90в†’- **AND** SHALL display that the package is already installed
    91в†’
    92в†’#### Scenario: Package updates
    93в†’- **WHEN** running `brew bundle` with existing packages
    94в†’- **THEN** Homebrew SHALL upgrade outdated packages if specified in Brewfile options
    95в†’
    96в†’### Requirement: Homebrew Bootstrap
    97в†’Homebrew itself SHALL be installed via a pre-hook if not already present.
    98в†’
    99в†’#### Scenario: Homebrew missing
   100в†’- **WHEN** chezmoi's pre-hook runs and Homebrew is not installed
   101в†’- **THEN** the hook SHALL install Homebrew before proceeding
   102в†’
   103в†’#### Scenario: Homebrew exists
   104в†’- **WHEN** Homebrew is already installed
   105в†’- **THEN** the pre-hook SHALL skip installation and continue
   106в†’
   107в†’### Requirement: Package Categories
   108в†’Packages SHALL be organized into logical categories based on their purpose.
   109в†’
   110в†’#### Scenario: Core packages
   111в†’- **WHEN** packages are essential for basic system operation
   112в†’- **THEN** they SHALL be placed in the `core` tag category
   113в†’
   114в†’#### Scenario: Development packages
   115в†’- **WHEN** packages are development tools (docker, IDEs, language tools)
   116в†’- **THEN** they SHALL be placed in the `dev` tag category
   117в†’
   118в†’#### Scenario: AI/ML packages
   119в†’- **WHEN** packages are AI or machine learning related (Claude, Ollama, LM Studio)
   120в†’- **THEN** they SHALL be placed in the `ai` tag category
   121в†’
   122в†’#### Scenario: Work packages
   123в†’- **WHEN** packages are enterprise or work-specific tools (Teams, Workspaces)
   124в†’- **THEN** they SHALL be placed in the `work` tag category
   125в†’
   126в†’#### Scenario: Personal packages
   127в†’- **WHEN** packages are personal productivity tools
   128в†’- **THEN** they SHALL be placed in the `personal` tag category
   129в†’
   130в†’#### Scenario: Data science packages
   131в†’- **WHEN** packages are data analysis tools (R, RStudio, csvkit)
   132в†’- **THEN** they SHALL be placed in the `datascience` tag category
   133в†’
   134в†’#### Scenario: Mobile/network packages
   135в†’- **WHEN** packages are VPN or network tools (Tunnelblick, WireGuard)
   136в†’- **THEN** they SHALL be placed in the `mobile` tag category
   137в†’
   138в†’### Requirement: Static Package Data File
   139в†’The `packages.yaml` file SHALL be a static file, not a template.
   140в†’
   141в†’#### Scenario: Pre-template availability
   142в†’- **WHEN** chezmoi's template engine initializes
   143в†’- **THEN** `packages.yaml` MUST exist and be parseable
   144в†’- **AND** SHALL NOT require template processing
   145в†’
   146в†’### Requirement: Claude Code Native Installation
   147в†’The system SHALL support native installation of Claude Code (Anthropic's AI coding assistant) when the `ai` tag is selected.
   148в†’
   149в†’#### Scenario: Fresh Claude Code installation on macOS
   150в†’- **WHEN** chezmoi applies configuration on macOS
   151в†’- **AND** the `ai` tag is selected
   152в†’- **AND** Claude Code is not already installed
   153в†’- **THEN** the installation script SHALL execute `curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash`
   154в†’- **AND** SHALL verify installation by running `claude --version`
   155в†’- **AND** SHALL display success message upon completion
   156в†’
   157в†’#### Scenario: Claude Code already installed
   158в†’- **WHEN** the installation script runs
   159в†’- **AND** the `claude` command is already available in PATH
   160в†’- **THEN** the script SHALL skip installation
   161в†’- **AND** SHALL display a skip message indicating Claude Code is already present
   162в†’- **AND** SHALL exit successfully without errors
   163в†’
   164в†’#### Scenario: Installation without ai tag
   165в†’- **WHEN** chezmoi applies configuration
   166в†’- **AND** the `ai` tag is NOT selected
   167в†’- **THEN** the Claude Code installation script SHALL not execute
   168в†’- **AND** no Claude Code installation SHALL occur
   169в†’
   170в†’#### Scenario: Platform detection for installation
   171в†’- **WHEN** the installation script runs
   172в†’- **AND** the platform is macOS (darwin)
   173в†’- **THEN** the script SHALL use the native install command for Unix-like systems
   174в†’- **AND** SHALL install the binary to `~/.local/bin/claude`
   175в†’
   176в†’#### Scenario: Linux/WSL installation support
   177в†’- **WHEN** the installation script runs
   178в†’- **AND** the platform is Linux or WSL
   179в†’- **THEN** the script SHALL use the same native install command
   180в†’- **AND** SHALL support Ubuntu 20.04+, Debian 10+, and compatible distributions
   181в†’- **AND** SHALL install the binary to `~/.local/bin/claude`
   182в†’
   183в†’#### Scenario: Installation failure handling
   184в†’- **WHEN** the Claude Code installation command fails
   185в†’- **THEN** the script SHALL display an error message using `print_message "error"`
   186в†’- **AND** SHALL exit with a non-zero status code
   187в†’- **AND** SHALL provide troubleshooting guidance
   188в†’
   189в†’#### Scenario: Post-installation verification
   190в†’- **WHEN** Claude Code installation completes successfully
   191в†’- **THEN** the script SHALL verify the `claude` command is available
   192в†’- **AND** SHALL run `claude --version` to confirm successful installation
   193в†’- **AND** SHALL display the installed version number
   194в†’
   195в†’### Requirement: Claude Code Script Execution Order
   196в†’The Claude Code installation script SHALL execute in the proper sequence within the development toolchain setup.
   197в†’
   198в†’#### Scenario: Script execution order
   199в†’- **WHEN** chezmoi runs installation scripts
   200в†’- **THEN** Claude Code installation (order 36) SHALL execute:
   201в†’  - **AFTER** nvm installation (order 35)
   202в†’  - **BEFORE** shell plugins and authentication (order 40+)
   203в†’- **AND** SHALL be positioned in the "Environment Managers" category (30-39)
   204в†’
   205в†’#### Scenario: Installation dependencies
   206в†’- **WHEN** the Claude Code installation script executes
   207в†’- **THEN** the system SHALL have curl available (from core packages)
   208в†’- **AND** SHALL have bash available (system default)
   209в†’- **AND** SHALL NOT require Node.js or other language runtimes
   210в†’
   211в†’### Requirement: Claude Code Integration with Shared Utilities
   212в†’The Claude Code installation script SHALL use the shared utility functions for consistent messaging and behavior.
   213в†’
   214в†’#### Scenario: Shared utility usage
   215в†’- **WHEN** the Claude Code installation script executes
   216в†’- **THEN** it SHALL source `scripts/shared-utils.sh`
   217в†’- **AND** SHALL use `print_message()` for all user-facing output
   218в†’- **AND** SHALL follow standard messaging conventions (info, success, warning, error, skip)
   219в†’
   220в†’#### Scenario: Idempotent execution
   221в†’- **WHEN** the Claude Code installation script runs multiple times
   222в†’- **THEN** it SHALL safely detect existing installations
   223в†’- **AND** SHALL not cause errors or duplicate installations
   224в†’- **AND** SHALL maintain system stability
   225в†’
   226в†’### Requirement: Claude Code Configuration Management
   227в†’The system SHALL provide Claude Code with proper configuration and state management.
   228в†’
   229в†’#### Scenario: Installation directory structure
   230в†’- **WHEN** Claude Code is installed via the native method
   231в†’- **THEN** the binary SHALL be placed at `~/.local/bin/claude`
   232в†’- **AND** configuration SHALL be stored in `~/.claude-code/`
   233в†’- **AND** user settings SHALL be stored in `~/.claude/`
   234в†’
   235в†’#### Scenario: Auto-update behavior
   236в†’- **WHEN** Claude Code is installed
   237в†’- **THEN** auto-updates SHALL be enabled by default
   238в†’- **AND** updates SHALL check on startup and periodically while running
   239в†’- **AND** users MAY disable auto-updates via `DISABLE_AUTOUPDATER=1` environment variable
   240в†’
   241в†’#### Scenario: PATH configuration
   242в†’- **WHEN** Claude Code is installed to `~/.local/bin/`
   243в†’- **THEN** the directory SHALL be included in the user's PATH
   244в†’- **AND** the `claude` command SHALL be immediately accessible
   245в†’- **AND** SHALL work without requiring shell restart
   246в†’
   247в†’## UV Tool Management
   248в†’
   249в†’### Requirement: UV Installation
   250в†’The `uv` package manager SHALL be installed before UV tools are installed.
   251в†’
   252в†’#### Scenario: UV not present
   253в†’- **WHEN** chezmoi applies configuration and `uv` is not installed
   254в†’- **THEN** the system SHALL install `uv` via the official Astral.sh installer
   255в†’- **AND** SHALL run at position 30 in the script execution order
   256в†’
   257в†’#### Scenario: UV already installed
   258в†’- **WHEN** `uv` is already present on the system
   259в†’- **THEN** the installation script SHALL skip reinstallation
   260в†’
   261в†’### Requirement: UV Tool Definition
   262в†’UV tools SHALL be defined within tag categories in `home/.chezmoidata/packages.yaml` using the `uv` key.
   263в†’
   264в†’#### Scenario: Tool organization structure
   265в†’- **WHEN** tools are defined in `packages.yaml`
   266в†’- **THEN** the YAML structure SHALL include `packages.darwin.<tag>.uv` with lists of tool specifications
   267в†’
   268в†’#### Scenario: Tool specification formats
   269в†’- **WHEN** a tool is defined
   270в†’- **THEN** it SHALL use one of these formats:
   271в†’  - Simple package name: `"package-name"`
   272в†’  - Git repository: `"git+https://github.com/org/repo@latest"`
   273в†’  - Version-pinned: `"package-name==1.0.0"`
   274в†’
   275в†’### Requirement: Tag-Based UV Tool Selection
   276в†’UV tool installation SHALL be controlled by user-selected tags.
   277в†’
   278в†’#### Scenario: Tag-conditional tools
   279в†’- **WHEN** a user selects the `ai` tag
   280в†’- **THEN** tools in the `ai` tag's `uv` list SHALL be installed
   281в†’- **AND** tools in unselected tag categories SHALL be skipped
   282в†’
   283в†’#### Scenario: Multiple tool categories
   284в†’- **WHEN** a user selects tags `core,dev,ai`
   285в†’- **THEN** UV tools from all three tag categories SHALL be installed via `uv tool install`
   286в†’
   287в†’### Requirement: UV Tool Installation Script
   288в†’UV tools SHALL be installed via a `run_onchange_before` script at position 25.
   289в†’
   290в†’#### Scenario: Tool installation execution
   291в†’- **WHEN** the tool installation script runs
   292в†’- **THEN** it SHALL execute `uv tool install <tool>` for each selected tool
   293в†’- **AND** SHALL re-run whenever the script content or `packages.yaml` changes
   294в†’
   295в†’#### Scenario: Tool installation idempotency
   296в†’- **WHEN** a tool is already installed
   297в†’- **THEN** `uv tool install` SHALL skip reinstallation or upgrade the tool
   298в†’- **AND** SHALL continue with remaining tools
   299в†’
   300в†’### Requirement: UV Tool Categories
   301в†’UV tools SHALL be organized within the same tag categories as Homebrew packages in `packages.yaml`.
   302в†’
   303в†’#### Scenario: AI tool category
   304в†’- **WHEN** a tool is AI-related (claude-monitor, zsh-llm-suggestions)
   305в†’- **THEN** it SHALL be placed under the `ai` tag's `uv` list in `packages.yaml`
   306в†’
   307в†’#### Scenario: Development tool category
   308в†’- **WHEN** a tool is development-related (linters, formatters, build tools)
   309в†’- **THEN** it SHALL be placed under the `dev` tag's `uv` list in `packages.yaml`
   310в†’
   311в†’#### Scenario: Core tool category
   312в†’- **WHEN** a tool is essential for basic operations
   313в†’- **THEN** it SHALL be placed under the `core` tag's `uv` list in `packages.yaml`
   314в†’
   315в†’## SDKMAN SDK Management
   316в†’
   317в†’### Requirement: SDKMAN Installation
   318в†’SDKMAN SHALL be installed before SDKs are installed, and only on machines with the `dev` tag.
   319в†’
   320в†’#### Scenario: SDKMAN installation with dev tag
   321в†’- **WHEN** chezmoi applies configuration with the `dev` tag selected
   322в†’- **THEN** the system SHALL install SDKMAN via the official installer
   323в†’- **AND** SHALL run at position 20 in the script execution order
   324в†’
   325в†’#### Scenario: SDKMAN skipped without dev tag
   326в†’- **WHEN** the `dev` tag is not selected
   327в†’- **THEN** SDKMAN installation SHALL be skipped entirely
   328в†’
   329в†’#### Scenario: SDKMAN already installed
   330в†’- **WHEN** SDKMAN is already present on the system
   331в†’- **THEN** the installation script SHALL skip reinstallation
   332в†’
   333в†’### Requirement: SDK Definition
   334в†’SDKs SHALL be defined within the `dev` tag in `home/.chezmoidata/packages.yaml` using the `sdkman` key.
   335в†’
   336в†’#### Scenario: SDK organization structure
   337в†’- **WHEN** SDKs are defined in `packages.yaml`
   338в†’- **THEN** the YAML structure SHALL include `packages.darwin.dev.sdkman` with a list of SDK specifications
   339в†’
   340в†’#### Scenario: SDK specification format
   341в†’- **WHEN** an SDK is defined
   342в†’- **THEN** it SHALL use the format: `"sdk-name version-identifier"`
   343в†’- **EXAMPLE**: `"java 25-tem"`, `"java 8.0.462-zulu"`, `"liquibase"`
   344в†’
   345в†’### Requirement: Tag-Based SDK Selection
   346в†’SDK installation SHALL require the `dev` tag.
   347в†’
   348в†’#### Scenario: Dev tag required
   349в†’- **WHEN** SDKs are being installed
   350в†’- **THEN** the `dev` tag MUST be selected
   351в†’- **AND** the installation script SHALL skip execution if `dev` tag is not present
   352в†’
   353в†’#### Scenario: Development SDK category
   354в†’- **WHEN** SDKs are listed under `packages.darwin.dev.sdkman`
   355в†’- **THEN** they SHALL be installed when the `dev` tag is selected
   356в†’
   357в†’### Requirement: SDK Installation Script
   358в†’SDKs SHALL be installed via a `run_onchange_before` script at position 24.
   359в†’
   360в†’#### Scenario: SDK installation execution
   361в†’- **WHEN** the SDK installation script runs with `dev` tag
   362в†’- **THEN** it SHALL execute `sdk install <sdk> <version>` for each defined SDK
   363в†’- **AND** SHALL re-run whenever the script content or `packages.yaml` changes
   364в†’
   365в†’#### Scenario: SDK installation idempotency
   366в†’- **WHEN** an SDK version is already installed
   367в†’- **THEN** `sdk install` SHALL skip reinstallation
   368в†’- **AND** SHALL display that the SDK is already installed
   369в†’
   370в†’### Requirement: SDKMAN Shell Integration
   371в†’SDKMAN SHALL be initialized in shell profiles to make SDKs available in interactive sessions.
   372в†’
   373в†’#### Scenario: Shell profile initialization
   374в†’- **WHEN** a shell profile (.bash_profile, .zshrc) is loaded
   375в†’- **THEN** it SHALL source `~/.sdkman/bin/sdkman-init.sh` if it exists
   376в†’- **AND** SHALL set `SDKMAN_DIR` environment variable
   377в†’
   378в†’#### Scenario: Conditional shell integration
   379в†’- **WHEN** the `dev` tag is not selected
   380в†’- **THEN** SDKMAN initialization SHALL NOT be included in shell profiles
   381в†’
   382в†’### Requirement: SDK Categories
   383в†’SDKs SHALL be defined under the `dev` tag's `sdkman` key in `packages.yaml`.
   384в†’
   385в†’#### Scenario: Java SDK versions
   386в†’- **WHEN** multiple Java versions are needed
   387в†’- **THEN** they SHALL be defined separately under `packages.darwin.dev.sdkman`
   388в†’- **EXAMPLE**: `"java 25-tem"` and `"java 8.0.462-zulu"`
   389в†’
   390в†’#### Scenario: Build tools
   391в†’- **WHEN** JVM build tools are needed (Liquibase, Gradle, Maven)
   392в†’- **THEN** they SHALL be placed under `packages.darwin.dev.sdkman` in `packages.yaml`
   393в†’
   394в†’## Script Execution Order
   395в†’
   396в†’### Requirement: Package Manager Installation Order
   397в†’Package managers SHALL be installed before their respective packages, following the numbered script execution sequence.
   398в†’
   399в†’#### Scenario: SDKMAN before SDKs
   400в†’- **WHEN** scripts execute in order
   401в†’- **THEN** SDKMAN installation (position 20) SHALL run before SDK installation (position 24)
   402в†’
   403в†’#### Scenario: Homebrew before packages
   404в†’- **WHEN** scripts execute in order
   405в†’- **THEN** Homebrew installation (pre-hook) SHALL run before package installation (position 23)
   406в†’
   407в†’#### Scenario: UV tool installation timing
   408в†’- **WHEN** scripts execute in order
   409в†’- **THEN** UV tool installation (position 25) runs before UV installation (position 30)
   410в†’- **NOTE**: This assumes UV is available through another mechanism (e.g., Homebrew in packages.yaml)
   411в†’
   412в†’### Requirement: Complete Installation Sequence
   413в†’The complete package installation sequence SHALL follow this order:
   414в†’
   415в†’#### Scenario: Full installation order
   416в†’- **WHEN** chezmoi applies configuration
   417в†’- **THEN** the installation sequence SHALL be:
   418в†’  1. Position 20: Install SDKMAN (if `dev` tag)
   419в†’  2. Position 23: Install Homebrew packages from packages.yaml
   420в†’  3. Position 24: Install SDKs via SDKMAN (if `dev` tag)
   421в†’  4. Position 25: Install tools via UV
   422в†’  5. Position 26: Install additional machine-specific Homebrew packages
   423в†’  6. Position 30: Install UV itself
   424в†’
   425в†’## Design Decisions
   426в†’
   427в†’### Three-Layer Package Management Rationale
   428в†’Using three complementary package management systems provides ecosystem-specific optimization:
   429в†’
   430в†’#### Homebrew (System Layer)
   431в†’- **Purpose**: System packages, GUI applications, fonts, CLI utilities
   432в†’- **Strengths**: Native macOS integration, wide package availability, cask support for applications
   433в†’- **Dual approach**: Essential tag-based packages + machine-specific Brewfiles
   434в†’- **Flexibility**: Users can add machine-specific packages without modifying the main repository
   435в†’
   436в†’#### UV Tools (Python Ecosystem)
   437в†’- **Purpose**: Python-based CLI tools and utilities
   438в†’- **Strengths**: Fast, modern Python package manager; isolated tool installations
   439в†’- **Pattern**: Tag-based selection with `core` always installed
   440в†’- **Use cases**: AI tools (claude-monitor), shell utilities (zsh-llm-suggestions), Python dev tools
   441в†’
   442в†’#### SDKMAN (JVM Ecosystem)
   443в†’- **Purpose**: Java SDKs, build tools, and JVM-related packages
   444в†’- **Strengths**: Version management for multiple Java installations, JVM ecosystem focus
   445в†’- **Pattern**: Requires `dev` tag, platform-specific definitions
   446в†’- **Use cases**: Multiple Java versions, Gradle, Maven, Liquibase, other JVM tools
   447в†’
   448в†’### Ecosystem Separation Benefits
   449в†’Separating package management by ecosystem provides:
   450в†’- **Optimized tools**: Each package manager specializes in its ecosystem
   451в†’- **Version isolation**: Multiple Java versions (SDKMAN), isolated Python tools (UV)
   452в†’- **Reduced conflicts**: System packages don't interfere with language-specific tools
   453в†’- **Clear responsibility**: Each layer has a well-defined purpose
   454в†’
   455в†’### Tag-Based Installation
   456в†’Using tags for package selection enables:
   457в†’- Customized installations for different machine purposes (work vs. personal vs. development)
   458в†’- Easy addition of new package categories
   459в†’- Minimal setup reduces installation time and disk usage
   460в†’- Clear categorization of package purposes
   461в†’
   462в†’### Machine-Specific Brewfiles
   463в†’Separate Brewfiles per machine allow:
   464в†’- Machine-specific tools without cluttering the main package list
   465в†’- User confirmation before installing potentially large or unwanted packages
   466в†’- Easy experimentation with new tools on one machine
   467в†’- Brewfile symlink enables manual `brew bundle` commands
   468в†’
   469в†’### Static Data File
   470в†’Making the package data file (`packages.yaml`) static ensures:
   471в†’- Available before template engine runs
   472в†’- Simple YAML editing without template syntax
   473в†’- Clear separation between data and logic
   474в†’- Easier for tools to parse and validate
   475в†’- Single source of truth for all package definitions
   476в†’
   477в†’### Script Execution Order Design
   478в†’The numbered script execution order (20, 23, 24, 25, 26, 30) uses 10-point range grouping:
   479в†’- **Categorical grouping**: Scripts in 20-29 range handle package management, 30-39 handle environment managers
   480в†’- **Flexible spacing**: Within each range, scripts use 1-5 point spacing as needed (20, 23, 24, 25, 26)
   481в†’- **Future expansion**: Gaps within ranges allow inserting new scripts without renumbering
   482в†’- **Dependency management**: Package managers install before their packages
   483в†’- **Predictable order**: Clear, documented sequence for troubleshooting
   484в†’
   485в†’**Note on UV timing**: Position 25 (UV tools) runs before position 30 (UV installation). This implies UV must be available through another mechanism (e.g., installed via Homebrew in packages.yaml).
   486в†’
   487в†’## Tag Reference
   488в†’
   489в†’### Tag Usage Across Package Managers
   490в†’
   491в†’This table shows which tags control package installation across all three package management systems:
   492в†’
   493в†’| Tag | Homebrew | UV Tools | SDKMAN | Description |
   494в†’|-----|----------|----------|--------|-------------|
   495в†’| `core` | вњ… Always | вњ… Always | вќЊ | Essential packages for basic system operation |
   496в†’| `dev` | вњ… Optional | вњ… Optional | вњ… **Required** | Development tools, IDEs, language toolchains |
   497в†’| `ai` | вњ… Optional | вњ… Optional | вќЊ | AI/ML tools (Claude, Ollama, LM Studio) |
   498в†’| `work` | вњ… Optional | вњ… Optional | вќЊ | Enterprise/work tools (Teams, Workspaces) |
   499в†’| `personal` | вњ… Optional | вњ… Optional | вќЊ | Personal productivity |
   500в†’| `datascience` | вњ… Optional | вњ… Optional | вќЊ | Data analysis tools (R, RStudio, csvkit) |
   501в†’| `mobile` | вњ… Optional | вњ… Optional | вќЊ | VPN and network tools (Tunnelblick, WireGuard) |
   502в†’
   503в†’### Tag Selection Behavior
   504в†’
   505в†’#### Core Tag (Always Active)
   506в†’- **Homebrew**: Core packages always installed
   507в†’- **UV Tools**: Core tools always installed
   508в†’- **SDKMAN**: Not applicable (requires `dev` tag)
   509в†’
   510в†’#### Development Tag (dev)
   511в†’- **Homebrew**: Installs development packages (Docker, IDEs, etc.)
   512в†’- **UV Tools**: Installs development tools
   513в†’- **SDKMAN**: **Required** - SDKMAN itself and all SDKs only install with `dev` tag
   514в†’
   515в†’#### Other Tags (ai, work, personal, datascience, mobile)
   516в†’- **Homebrew**: Each tag installs corresponding category packages
   517в†’- **UV Tools**: Each tag installs corresponding category tools
   518в†’- **SDKMAN**: Not used (only `dev` category exists)
   519в†’
   520в†’### Data File Structure
   521в†’
   522в†’#### packages.yaml (Unified Package Definition)
   523в†’```yaml
   524в†’packages:
   525в†’  darwin:
   526в†’    taps:
   527в†’    - 'isen-ng/dotnet-sdk-versions'
   528в†’    - 'buo/cask-upgrade'
   529в†’
   530в†’    core:
   531в†’      taps: [...]
   532в†’      brews: [...]
   533в†’      casks: [...]
   534в†’      mas: [...]
   535в†’
   536в†’    dev:
   537в†’      taps: [...]
   538в†’      brews: [...]
   539в†’      casks: [...]
   540в†’      mas: [...]
   541в†’      sdkman:       # SDKMAN SDKs (only in dev tag)
   542в†’      - "java 25-tem"
   543в†’      - "java 8.0.462-zulu"
   544в†’      - "liquibase"
   545в†’
   546в†’    ai:
   547в†’      taps: [...]
   548в†’      brews: [...]
   549в†’      casks: [...]
   550в†’      uv:           # UV Python tools (can be in any tag)
   551в†’      - "basic-memory"
   552в†’      - "claude-monitor"
   553в†’      - "git+https://github.com/org/repo@latest"
   554в†’
   555в†’    # ... other tags (work, personal, datascience, mobile)
   556в†’```
   557в†’
   558в†’### Installation Scripts
   559в†’
   560в†’| Script | Position | Frequency | Purpose |
   561в†’|--------|----------|-----------|---------|
   562в†’| `run_once_before_darwin-20-install-sdkman.sh.tmpl` | 20 | Once | Install SDKMAN (requires `dev` tag) |
   563в†’| `run_onchange_before_darwin-23-install-packages.sh.tmpl` | 23 | On change | Install Homebrew packages from packages.yaml |
   564в†’| `run_onchange_before_darwin-24-install-sdks.sh.tmpl` | 24 | On change | Install SDKs via SDKMAN from packages.yaml (requires `dev` tag) |
   565в†’| `run_onchange_before_darwin-25-install-tools.sh.tmpl` | 25 | On change | Install UV tools from packages.yaml |
   566в†’| `run_onchange_before_darwin-26-brew-bundle-install.sh.tmpl` | 26 | On change | Install machine-specific Homebrew packages |
   567в†’| `run_once_before_darwin-30-install-uv.sh.tmpl` | 30 | Once | Install UV package manager |
   568в†’
```

    44в†’{{ end -}}
    45в†’    {{- end }}

     1в†’{{- if and (eq .chezmoi.os "darwin") (has "dev" .tags) -}}
     2в†’#!/bin/bash
     3в†’
     4в†’set -euo pipefail
     5в†’
     6в†’source "{{ .chezmoi.sourceDir -}}/scripts/shared-utils.sh"
     7в†’
     8в†’if ! require_tools curl zip unzip; then
     9в†’    exit 1
    10в†’fi
    11в†’
    12в†’if [[ -d "$HOME/.sdkman" ]] && [[ -f "$HOME/.sdkman/bin/sdkman-init.sh" ]]; then
    13в†’    print_message "info" "sdkman is already installed. Updating..."
    14в†’    set +u  # disable nounset; sdkman scripts reference unbound variables (e.g., ZSH_VERSION)
    15в†’    source "$HOME/.sdkman/bin/sdkman-init.sh"
    16в†’    sdk update
    17в†’    set -u  # re-enable nounset
    18в†’    exit 0
    19в†’fi
    20в†’
    21в†’print_message "info" "Installing sdkman (Software Development Kit Manager)..."
    22в†’
    23в†’# Install sdkman using the official installation script from sdkman.io
    24в†’export SDKMAN_DIR="$HOME/.sdkman"
    25в†’curl -s "https://get.sdkman.io" | bash
    26в†’
    27в†’print_message "success" "sdkman installed successfully!"
    28в†’print_message "info" "sdkman installed to: $SDKMAN_DIR. You may need to restart your terminal to start using it."
    29в†’
    30в†’{{ end -}}

Perfect! Now I have a complete picture. Let me create a comprehensive summary document for the user.

## Complete Pattern: How `uv` Packages Are Installed

Based on my thorough search through your chezmoi dotfiles repository, here's the complete pattern for how `uv` packages flow from definition to installation:

### 1. Package Definition in `packages.yaml`

**File**: `/Users/craig/.local/share/chezmoi/home/.chezmoidata/packages.yaml` (lines 138-142)

```yaml
# In the "ai" tag section:
ai:
  taps: [...]
  brews: [...]
  casks: [...]
  uv:
  - "basic-memory"
  - "claude-monitor"
  - "git+https://github.com/cearley/claude-extend@latest"
  - "git+https://github.com/cearley/zsh-llm-suggestions"
```

**Key characteristics**:
- `uv` is a top-level key within any tag category (core, dev, ai, work, etc.)
- Supports three specification formats:
  - Simple package names: `"basic-memory"`
  - Git repositories: `"git+https://github.com/org/repo@latest"`
  - Version-pinned: `"package-name==1.0.0"`
- Currently only used in the `ai` tag; also works in other tags

### 2. Bun Packages Already Exist!

I found bun packages are **already defined** in your packages.yaml:

**File**: `/Users/craig/.local/share/chezmoi/home/.chezmoidata/packages.yaml` (lines 127-128)

```yaml
# In the "ai" tag section:
ai:
  bun:
  - 'ralph-tui'
```

However, **there is NO installation script for bun packages yet** - this is where the gap is.

### 3. UV Package Installation Script

**File**: `/Users/craig/.local/share/chezmoi/home/.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_before_darwin-25-install-tools.sh.tmpl`

```bash
{{ if eq .chezmoi.os "darwin" -}}
#!/bin/bash

source "{{ .chezmoi.sourceDir -}}/scripts/shared-utils.sh"

if ! require_tools uv; then
    exit 1
fi

print_message "info" "Installing tools using uv..."

{{- $categories := prepend .tag_choices "core" }}
{{- range $category := $categories }}
  {{- $categoryData := index $.packages.darwin $category }}
  {{- if or (eq $category "core") (has $category $.tags) }}
    {{- if and (hasKey $categoryData "uv") $categoryData.uv }}
# Install {{ $category }} tools
{{ range $categoryData.uv -}}
uv tool install {{ . }}
{{ end -}}
    {{- end }}
  {{- end }}
{{- end }}

{{ end -}}
```

**How it works**:
1. Runs only on macOS (`eq .chezmoi.os "darwin"`)
2. Sources shared utilities for consistent messaging
3. Requires `uv` to be already installed (position 30)
4. Uses Go template logic to:
   - Prepend "core" to tag choices
   - Iterate over each tag category
   - Check if category is "core" OR selected in user tags
   - Extract `uv` key from that category's data
   - Execute `uv tool install <package>` for each

**Key naming convention**: `run_onchange_before_darwin-25-install-tools.sh.tmpl`
- `run_onchange`: Re-runs when script or `packages.yaml` changes
- `before`: Runs before user scripts
- `darwin-25`: macOS platform, position 25
- Position 25 is specifically for "UV tool installation"

### 4. Complete Installation Sequence

```
Position 20: Install SDKMAN (if dev tag) в†ђ run_once_before
Position 23: Install Homebrew packages   в†ђ run_onchange_before
Position 24: Install SDKs via SDKMAN     в†ђ run_onchange_before
Position 25: Install UV tools            в†ђ run_onchange_before  <- bun should go here
Position 26: Install machine-specific Brewfiles в†ђ run_onchange_before
Position 30: Install UV itself           в†ђ run_once_before
```

### 5. Related Infrastructure: SDKMAN Pattern (for reference)

For comparison, here's how SDKMAN follows a similar pattern:

**Manager Installation** (position 20): `run_once_before_darwin-20-install-sdkman.sh.tmpl`
- Installs SDKMAN only once
- Conditional: `if and (eq .chezmoi.os "darwin") (has "dev" .tags)`

**Package Installation** (position 24): `run_onchange_before_darwin-24-install-sdks.sh.tmpl`
- Iterates through packages.yaml like UV script
- Sources init script if needed
- Executes `sdk install <package> <version>`

### 6. Package Management Spec Reference

From `/Users/craig/.local/share/chezmoi/openspec/specs/package-management/spec.md` (lines 287-314):

The spec defines exact requirements for UV tool management:
- Tools are defined in `packages.yaml` under tag categories with `uv` key
- Installation script runs at position 25 with `run_onchange_before` frequency
- Re-runs when script content or `packages.yaml` changes
- Idempotent: `uv tool install` handles already-installed packages
- Uses same tag-based selection as Homebrew (core always, others conditional)

```text
     1в†’# Package Management System
     2в†’
     3в†’## Purpose
     4в†’The package management system provides a unified approach for installing packages across different ecosystems, all defined in a single `packages.yaml` file:
     5в†’- **Homebrew**: System packages, applications, and CLI tools (essential tag-based + machine-specific)
     6в†’- **UV Tools**: Python-based CLI tools and utilities (tag-based, within `packages.yaml`)
     7в†’- **SDKMAN SDKs**: JVM ecosystem SDKs and build tools (tag-based, within `packages.yaml`, requires `dev` tag)
     8в†’
     9в†’## Requirements
    10в†’### Requirement: Central Package Definition
    11в†’All packages SHALL be defined in `home/.chezmoidata/packages.yaml` organized by platform and tag.
    12в†’
    13в†’#### Scenario: Platform-based package organization
    14в†’- **WHEN** packages are defined for the darwin platform
    15в†’- **THEN** the YAML structure SHALL include `packages.darwin` with nested tag categories
    16в†’
    17в†’#### Scenario: Tag-based package categories
    18в†’- **WHEN** packages are categorized by tags (core, dev, ai, work, personal, datascience, mobile)
    19в†’- **THEN** each tag SHALL contain separate lists for `taps`, `brews`, `casks`, `mas`, `sdkman`, and `uv` packages
    20в†’
    21в†’### Requirement: Tag-Based Package Selection
    22в†’Package installation SHALL be controlled by user-selected tags during chezmoi initialization.
    23в†’
    24в†’#### Scenario: Core packages always installed
    25в†’- **WHEN** chezmoi applies configuration
    26в†’- **THEN** packages in the `core` tag SHALL be installed regardless of other selected tags
    27в†’
    28в†’#### Scenario: Optional tag packages
    29в†’- **WHEN** a user selects the `dev` tag
    30в†’- **THEN** only packages in `core` and `dev` tags SHALL be installed
    31в†’- **AND** packages in unselected tags SHALL be skipped
    32в†’
    33в†’#### Scenario: Multiple tag selection
    34в†’- **WHEN** a user selects tags `core,dev,ai,personal`
    35в†’- **THEN** packages from all four tag categories SHALL be installed
    36в†’
    37в†’### Requirement: Homebrew Package Types
    38в†’The system SHALL support all Homebrew package types: taps, brews (formulae), casks, and Mac App Store apps.
    39в†’
    40в†’#### Scenario: Homebrew tap installation
    41в†’- **WHEN** a tap is defined in `packages.yaml` under the `taps` key
    42в†’- **THEN** the tap SHALL be added via `brew tap` before installing packages from that tap
    43в†’
    44в†’#### Scenario: Formula installation
    45в†’- **WHEN** a package is listed under `brews`
    46в†’- **THEN** it SHALL be installed via `brew install <package>`
    47в†’
    48в†’#### Scenario: Cask installation
    49в†’- **WHEN** an application is listed under `casks`
    50в†’- **THEN** it SHALL be installed via `brew install --cask <package>`
    51в†’
    52в†’#### Scenario: Mac App Store installation
    53в†’- **WHEN** an app is listed under `mas` with format `"App Name", id: 123456`
    54в†’- **THEN** it SHALL be installed via `mas install 123456`
    55в†’
    56в†’### Requirement: Machine-Specific Brewfiles
    57в†’Additional packages SHALL be defined in machine-specific Brewfiles located in the `brewfiles/` directory.
    58в†’
    59в†’#### Scenario: Machine-specific Brewfile location
    60в†’- **WHEN** the machine configuration specifies `brewfile: mbp-brewfile`
    61в†’- **THEN** the system SHALL use `brewfiles/mbp-brewfile` for additional packages
    62в†’
    63в†’#### Scenario: Brewfile symlinking
    64в†’- **WHEN** chezmoi applies configuration
    65в†’- **THEN** the machine-specific Brewfile SHALL be symlinked to `~/Brewfile`
    66в†’- **AND** SHALL be accessible for manual `brew bundle` operations
    67в†’
    68в†’### Requirement: User Confirmation for Additional Packages
    69в†’Machine-specific Brewfile packages SHALL require explicit user confirmation before installation.
    70в†’
    71в†’#### Scenario: Confirmation prompt
    72в†’- **WHEN** a machine-specific Brewfile contains additional packages
    73в†’- **THEN** the script SHALL prompt the user for confirmation before running `brew bundle`
    74в†’
    75в†’#### Scenario: Confirmation denied
    76в†’- **WHEN** the user declines the Brewfile installation prompt
    77в†’- **THEN** the script SHALL skip Brewfile installation
    78в†’- **AND** SHALL continue with other setup tasks
    79в†’
    80в†’#### Scenario: Confirmation accepted
    81в†’- **WHEN** the user confirms the Brewfile installation prompt
    82в†’- **THEN** the script SHALL execute `brew bundle --file ~/Brewfile`
    83в†’
    84в†’### Requirement: Idempotent Package Installation
    85в†’Package installation SHALL be idempotent and safe to re-run.
    86в†’
    87в†’#### Scenario: Already installed package
    88в†’- **WHEN** a package is already installed
    89в†’- **THEN** `brew install` SHALL skip reinstallation
    90в†’- **AND** SHALL display that the package is already installed
    91в†’
    92в†’#### Scenario: Package updates
    93в†’- **WHEN** running `brew bundle` with existing packages
    94в†’- **THEN** Homebrew SHALL upgrade outdated packages if specified in Brewfile options
    95в†’
    96в†’### Requirement: Homebrew Bootstrap
    97в†’Homebrew itself SHALL be installed via a pre-hook if not already present.
    98в†’
    99в†’#### Scenario: Homebrew missing
   100в†’- **WHEN** chezmoi's pre-hook runs and Homebrew is not installed
   101в†’- **THEN** the hook SHALL install Homebrew before proceeding
   102в†’
   103в†’#### Scenario: Homebrew exists
   104в†’- **WHEN** Homebrew is already installed
   105в†’- **THEN** the pre-hook SHALL skip installation and continue
   106в†’
   107в†’### Requirement: Package Categories
   108в†’Packages SHALL be organized into logical categories based on their purpose.
   109в†’
   110в†’#### Scenario: Core packages
   111в†’- **WHEN** packages are essential for basic system operation
   112в†’- **THEN** they SHALL be placed in the `core` tag category
   113в†’
   114в†’#### Scenario: Development packages
   115в†’- **WHEN** packages are development tools (docker, IDEs, language tools)
   116в†’- **THEN** they SHALL be placed in the `dev` tag category
   117в†’
   118в†’#### Scenario: AI/ML packages
   119в†’- **WHEN** packages are AI or machine learning related (Claude, Ollama, LM Studio)
   120в†’- **THEN** they SHALL be placed in the `ai` tag category
   121в†’
   122в†’#### Scenario: Work packages
   123в†’- **WHEN** packages are enterprise or work-specific tools (Teams, Workspaces)
   124в†’- **THEN** they SHALL be placed in the `work` tag category
   125в†’
   126в†’#### Scenario: Personal packages
   127в†’- **WHEN** packages are personal productivity tools
   128в†’- **THEN** they SHALL be placed in the `personal` tag category
   129в†’
   130в†’#### Scenario: Data science packages
   131в†’- **WHEN** packages are data analysis tools (R, RStudio, csvkit)
   132в†’- **THEN** they SHALL be placed in the `datascience` tag category
   133в†’
   134в†’#### Scenario: Mobile/network packages
   135в†’- **WHEN** packages are VPN or network tools (Tunnelblick, WireGuard)
   136в†’- **THEN** they SHALL be placed in the `mobile` tag category
   137в†’
   138в†’### Requirement: Static Package Data File
   139в†’The `packages.yaml` file SHALL be a static file, not a template.
   140в†’
   141в†’#### Scenario: Pre-template availability
   142в†’- **WHEN** chezmoi's template engine initializes
   143в†’- **THEN** `packages.yaml` MUST exist and be parseable
   144в†’- **AND** SHALL NOT require template processing
   145в†’
   146в†’### Requirement: Claude Code Native Installation
   147в†’The system SHALL support native installation of Claude Code (Anthropic's AI coding assistant) when the `ai` tag is selected.
   148в†’
   149в†’#### Scenario: Fresh Claude Code installation on macOS
   150в†’- **WHEN** chezmoi applies configuration on macOS
   151в†’- **AND** the `ai` tag is selected
   152в†’- **AND** Claude Code is not already installed
   153в†’- **THEN** the installation script SHALL execute `curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash`
   154в†’- **AND** SHALL verify installation by running `claude --version`
   155в†’- **AND** SHALL display success message upon completion
   156в†’
   157в†’#### Scenario: Claude Code already installed
   158в†’- **WHEN** the installation script runs
   159в†’- **AND** the `claude` command is already available in PATH
   160в†’- **THEN** the script SHALL skip installation
   161в†’- **AND** SHALL display a skip message indicating Claude Code is already present
   162в†’- **AND** SHALL exit successfully without errors
   163в†’
   164в†’#### Scenario: Installation without ai tag
   165в†’- **WHEN** chezmoi applies configuration
   166в†’- **AND** the `ai` tag is NOT selected
   167в†’- **THEN** the Claude Code installation script SHALL not execute
   168в†’- **AND** no Claude Code installation SHALL occur
   169в†’
   170в†’#### Scenario: Platform detection for installation
   171в†’- **WHEN** the installation script runs
   172в†’- **AND** the platform is macOS (darwin)
   173в†’- **THEN** the script SHALL use the native install command for Unix-like systems
   174в†’- **AND** SHALL install the binary to `~/.local/bin/claude`
   175в†’
   176в†’#### Scenario: Linux/WSL installation support
   177в†’- **WHEN** the installation script runs
   178в†’- **AND** the platform is Linux or WSL
   179в†’- **THEN** the script SHALL use the same native install command
   180в†’- **AND** SHALL support Ubuntu 20.04+, Debian 10+, and compatible distributions
   181в†’- **AND** SHALL install the binary to `~/.local/bin/claude`
   182в†’
   183в†’#### Scenario: Installation failure handling
   184в†’- **WHEN** the Claude Code installation command fails
   185в†’- **THEN** the script SHALL display an error message using `print_message "error"`
   186в†’- **AND** SHALL exit with a non-zero status code
   187в†’- **AND** SHALL provide troubleshooting guidance
   188в†’
   189в†’#### Scenario: Post-installation verification
   190в†’- **WHEN** Claude Code installation completes successfully
   191в†’- **THEN** the script SHALL verify the `claude` command is available
   192в†’- **AND** SHALL run `claude --version` to confirm successful installation
   193в†’- **AND** SHALL display the installed version number
   194в†’
   195в†’### Requirement: Claude Code Script Execution Order
   196в†’The Claude Code installation script SHALL execute in the proper sequence within the development toolchain setup.
   197в†’
   198в†’#### Scenario: Script execution order
   199в†’- **WHEN** chezmoi runs installation scripts
   200в†’- **THEN** Claude Code installation (order 36) SHALL execute:
   201в†’  - **AFTER** nvm installation (order 35)
   202в†’  - **BEFORE** shell plugins and authentication (order 40+)
   203в†’- **AND** SHALL be positioned in the "Environment Managers" category (30-39)
   204в†’
   205в†’#### Scenario: Installation dependencies
   206в†’- **WHEN** the Claude Code installation script executes
   207в†’- **THEN** the system SHALL have curl available (from core packages)
   208в†’- **AND** SHALL have bash available (system default)
   209в†’- **AND** SHALL NOT require Node.js or other language runtimes
   210в†’
   211в†’### Requirement: Claude Code Integration with Shared Utilities
   212в†’The Claude Code installation script SHALL use the shared utility functions for consistent messaging and behavior.
   213в†’
   214в†’#### Scenario: Shared utility usage
   215в†’- **WHEN** the Claude Code installation script executes
   216в†’- **THEN** it SHALL source `scripts/shared-utils.sh`
   217в†’- **AND** SHALL use `print_message()` for all user-facing output
   218в†’- **AND** SHALL follow standard messaging conventions (info, success, warning, error, skip)
   219в†’
   220в†’#### Scenario: Idempotent execution
   221в†’- **WHEN** the Claude Code installation script runs multiple times
   222в†’- **THEN** it SHALL safely detect existing installations
   223в†’- **AND** SHALL not cause errors or duplicate installations
   224в†’- **AND** SHALL maintain system stability
   225в†’
   226в†’### Requirement: Claude Code Configuration Management
   227в†’The system SHALL provide Claude Code with proper configuration and state management.
   228в†’
   229в†’#### Scenario: Installation directory structure
   230в†’- **WHEN** Claude Code is installed via the native method
   231в†’- **THEN** the binary SHALL be placed at `~/.local/bin/claude`
   232в†’- **AND** configuration SHALL be stored in `~/.claude-code/`
   233в†’- **AND** user settings SHALL be stored in `~/.claude/`
   234в†’
   235в†’#### Scenario: Auto-update behavior
   236в†’- **WHEN** Claude Code is installed
   237в†’- **THEN** auto-updates SHALL be enabled by default
   238в†’- **AND** updates SHALL check on startup and periodically while running
   239в†’- **AND** users MAY disable auto-updates via `DISABLE_AUTOUPDATER=1` environment variable
   240в†’
   241в†’#### Scenario: PATH configuration
   242в†’- **WHEN** Claude Code is installed to `~/.local/bin/`
   243в†’- **THEN** the directory SHALL be included in the user's PATH
   244в†’- **AND** the `claude` command SHALL be immediately accessible
   245в†’- **AND** SHALL work without requiring shell restart
   246в†’
   247в†’## UV Tool Management
   248в†’
   249в†’### Requirement: UV Installation
   250в†’The `uv` package manager SHALL be installed before UV tools are installed.
   251в†’
   252в†’#### Scenario: UV not present
   253в†’- **WHEN** chezmoi applies configuration and `uv` is not installed
   254в†’- **THEN** the system SHALL install `uv` via the official Astral.sh installer
   255в†’- **AND** SHALL run at position 30 in the script execution order
   256в†’
   257в†’#### Scenario: UV already installed
   258в†’- **WHEN** `uv` is already present on the system
   259в†’- **THEN** the installation script SHALL skip reinstallation
   260в†’
   261в†’### Requirement: UV Tool Definition
   262в†’UV tools SHALL be defined within tag categories in `home/.chezmoidata/packages.yaml` using the `uv` key.
   263в†’
   264в†’#### Scenario: Tool organization structure
   265в†’- **WHEN** tools are defined in `packages.yaml`
   266в†’- **THEN** the YAML structure SHALL include `packages.darwin.<tag>.uv` with lists of tool specifications
   267в†’
   268в†’#### Scenario: Tool specification formats
   269в†’- **WHEN** a tool is defined
   270в†’- **THEN** it SHALL use one of these formats:
   271в†’  - Simple package name: `"package-name"`
   272в†’  - Git repository: `"git+https://github.com/org/repo@latest"`
   273в†’  - Version-pinned: `"package-name==1.0.0"`
   274в†’
   275в†’### Requirement: Tag-Based UV Tool Selection
   276в†’UV tool installation SHALL be controlled by user-selected tags.
   277в†’
   278в†’#### Scenario: Tag-conditional tools
   279в†’- **WHEN** a user selects the `ai` tag
   280в†’- **THEN** tools in the `ai` tag's `uv` list SHALL be installed
   281в†’- **AND** tools in unselected tag categories SHALL be skipped
   282в†’
   283в†’#### Scenario: Multiple tool categories
   284в†’- **WHEN** a user selects tags `core,dev,ai`
   285в†’- **THEN** UV tools from all three tag categories SHALL be installed via `uv tool install`
   286в†’
   287в†’### Requirement: UV Tool Installation Script
   288в†’UV tools SHALL be installed via a `run_onchange_before` script at position 25.
   289в†’
   290в†’#### Scenario: Tool installation execution
   291в†’- **WHEN** the tool installation script runs
   292в†’- **THEN** it SHALL execute `uv tool install <tool>` for each selected tool
   293в†’- **AND** SHALL re-run whenever the script content or `packages.yaml` changes
   294в†’
   295в†’#### Scenario: Tool installation idempotency
   296в†’- **WHEN** a tool is already installed
   297в†’- **THEN** `uv tool install` SHALL skip reinstallation or upgrade the tool
   298в†’- **AND** SHALL continue with remaining tools
   299в†’
   300в†’### Requirement: UV Tool Categories
   301в†’UV tools SHALL be organized within the same tag categories as Homebrew packages in `packages.yaml`.
   302в†’
   303в†’#### Scenario: AI tool category
   304в†’- **WHEN** a tool is AI-related (claude-monitor, zsh-llm-suggestions)
   305в†’- **THEN** it SHALL be placed under the `ai` tag's `uv` list in `packages.yaml`
   306в†’
   307в†’#### Scenario: Development tool category
   308в†’- **WHEN** a tool is development-related (linters, formatters, build tools)
   309в†’- **THEN** it SHALL be placed under the `dev` tag's `uv` list in `packages.yaml`
   310в†’
   311в†’#### Scenario: Core tool category
   312в†’- **WHEN** a tool is essential for basic operations
   313в†’- **THEN** it SHALL be placed under the `core` tag's `uv` list in `packages.yaml`
   314в†’
   315в†’## SDKMAN SDK Management
   316в†’
   317в†’### Requirement: SDKMAN Installation
   318в†’SDKMAN SHALL be installed before SDKs are installed, and only on machines with the `dev` tag.
   319в†’
   320в†’#### Scenario: SDKMAN installation with dev tag
   321в†’- **WHEN** chezmoi applies configuration with the `dev` tag selected
   322в†’- **THEN** the system SHALL install SDKMAN via the official installer
   323в†’- **AND** SHALL run at position 20 in the script execution order
   324в†’
   325в†’#### Scenario: SDKMAN skipped without dev tag
   326в†’- **WHEN** the `dev` tag is not selected
   327в†’- **THEN** SDKMAN installation SHALL be skipped entirely
   328в†’
   329в†’#### Scenario: SDKMAN already installed
   330в†’- **WHEN** SDKMAN is already present on the system
   331в†’- **THEN** the installation script SHALL skip reinstallation
   332в†’
   333в†’### Requirement: SDK Definition
   334в†’SDKs SHALL be defined within the `dev` tag in `home/.chezmoidata/packages.yaml` using the `sdkman` key.
   335в†’
   336в†’#### Scenario: SDK organization structure
   337в†’- **WHEN** SDKs are defined in `packages.yaml`
   338в†’- **THEN** the YAML structure SHALL include `packages.darwin.dev.sdkman` with a list of SDK specifications
   339в†’
   340в†’#### Scenario: SDK specification format
   341в†’- **WHEN** an SDK is defined
   342в†’- **THEN** it SHALL use the format: `"sdk-name version-identifier"`
   343в†’- **EXAMPLE**: `"java 25-tem"`, `"java 8.0.462-zulu"`, `"liquibase"`
   344в†’
   345в†’### Requirement: Tag-Based SDK Selection
   346в†’SDK installation SHALL require the `dev` tag.
   347в†’
   348в†’#### Scenario: Dev tag required
   349в†’- **WHEN** SDKs are being installed
   350в†’- **THEN** the `dev` tag MUST be selected
   351в†’- **AND** the installation script SHALL skip execution if `dev` tag is not present
   352в†’
   353в†’#### Scenario: Development SDK category
   354в†’- **WHEN** SDKs are listed under `packages.darwin.dev.sdkman`
   355в†’- **THEN** they SHALL be installed when the `dev` tag is selected
   356в†’
   357в†’### Requirement: SDK Installation Script
   358в†’SDKs SHALL be installed via a `run_onchange_before` script at position 24.
   359в†’
   360в†’#### Scenario: SDK installation execution
   361в†’- **WHEN** the SDK installation script runs with `dev` tag
   362в†’- **THEN** it SHALL execute `sdk install <sdk> <version>` for each defined SDK
   363в†’- **AND** SHALL re-run whenever the script content or `packages.yaml` changes
   364в†’
   365в†’#### Scenario: SDK installation idempotency
   366в†’- **WHEN** an SDK version is already installed
   367в†’- **THEN** `sdk install` SHALL skip reinstallation
   368в†’- **AND** SHALL display that the SDK is already installed
   369в†’
   370в†’### Requirement: SDKMAN Shell Integration
   371в†’SDKMAN SHALL be initialized in shell profiles to make SDKs available in interactive sessions.
   372в†’
   373в†’#### Scenario: Shell profile initialization
   374в†’- **WHEN** a shell profile (.bash_profile, .zshrc) is loaded
   375в†’- **THEN** it SHALL source `~/.sdkman/bin/sdkman-init.sh` if it exists
   376в†’- **AND** SHALL set `SDKMAN_DIR` environment variable
   377в†’
   378в†’#### Scenario: Conditional shell integration
   379в†’- **WHEN** the `dev` tag is not selected
   380в†’- **THEN** SDKMAN initialization SHALL NOT be included in shell profiles
   381в†’
   382в†’### Requirement: SDK Categories
   383в†’SDKs SHALL be defined under the `dev` tag's `sdkman` key in `packages.yaml`.
   384в†’
   385в†’#### Scenario: Java SDK versions
   386в†’- **WHEN** multiple Java versions are needed
   387в†’- **THEN** they SHALL be defined separately under `packages.darwin.dev.sdkman`
   388в†’- **EXAMPLE**: `"java 25-tem"` and `"java 8.0.462-zulu"`
   389в†’
   390в†’#### Scenario: Build tools
   391в†’- **WHEN** JVM build tools are needed (Liquibase, Gradle, Maven)
   392в†’- **THEN** they SHALL be placed under `packages.darwin.dev.sdkman` in `packages.yaml`
   393в†’
   394в†’## Script Execution Order
   395в†’
   396в†’### Requirement: Package Manager Installation Order
   397в†’Package managers SHALL be installed before their respective packages, following the numbered script execution sequence.
   398в†’
   399в†’#### Scenario: SDKMAN before SDKs
   400в†’- **WHEN** scripts execute in order
   401в†’- **THEN** SDKMAN installation (position 20) SHALL run before SDK installation (position 24)
   402в†’
   403в†’#### Scenario: Homebrew before packages
   404в†’- **WHEN** scripts execute in order
   405в†’- **THEN** Homebrew installation (pre-hook) SHALL run before package installation (position 23)
   406в†’
   407в†’#### Scenario: UV tool installation timing
   408в†’- **WHEN** scripts execute in order
   409в†’- **THEN** UV tool installation (position 25) runs before UV installation (position 30)
   410в†’- **NOTE**: This assumes UV is available through another mechanism (e.g., Homebrew in packages.yaml)
   411в†’
   412в†’### Requirement: Complete Installation Sequence
   413в†’The complete package installation sequence SHALL follow this order:
   414в†’
   415в†’#### Scenario: Full installation order
   416в†’- **WHEN** chezmoi applies configuration
   417в†’- **THEN** the installation sequence SHALL be:
   418в†’  1. Position 20: Install SDKMAN (if `dev` tag)
   419в†’  2. Position 23: Install Homebrew packages from packages.yaml
   420в†’  3. Position 24: Install SDKs via SDKMAN (if `dev` tag)
   421в†’  4. Position 25: Install tools via UV
   422в†’  5. Position 26: Install additional machine-specific Homebrew packages
   423в†’  6. Position 30: Install UV itself
   424в†’
   425в†’## Design Decisions
   426в†’
   427в†’### Three-Layer Package Management Rationale
   428в†’Using three complementary package management systems provides ecosystem-specific optimization:
   429в†’
   430в†’#### Homebrew (System Layer)
   431в†’- **Purpose**: System packages, GUI applications, fonts, CLI utilities
   432в†’- **Strengths**: Native macOS integration, wide package availability, cask support for applications
   433в†’- **Dual approach**: Essential tag-based packages + machine-specific Brewfiles
   434в†’- **Flexibility**: Users can add machine-specific packages without modifying the main repository
   435в†’
   436в†’#### UV Tools (Python Ecosystem)
   437в†’- **Purpose**: Python-based CLI tools and utilities
   438в†’- **Strengths**: Fast, modern Python package manager; isolated tool installations
   439в†’- **Pattern**: Tag-based selection with `core` always installed
   440в†’- **Use cases**: AI tools (claude-monitor), shell utilities (zsh-llm-suggestions), Python dev tools
   441в†’
   442в†’#### SDKMAN (JVM Ecosystem)
   443в†’- **Purpose**: Java SDKs, build tools, and JVM-related packages
   444в†’- **Strengths**: Version management for multiple Java installations, JVM ecosystem focus
   445в†’- **Pattern**: Requires `dev` tag, platform-specific definitions
   446в†’- **Use cases**: Multiple Java versions, Gradle, Maven, Liquibase, other JVM tools
   447в†’
   448в†’### Ecosystem Separation Benefits
   449в†’Separating package management by ecosystem provides:
   450в†’- **Optimized tools**: Each package manager specializes in its ecosystem
   451в†’- **Version isolation**: Multiple Java versions (SDKMAN), isolated Python tools (UV)
   452в†’- **Reduced conflicts**: System packages don't interfere with language-specific tools
   453в†’- **Clear responsibility**: Each layer has a well-defined purpose
   454в†’
   455в†’### Tag-Based Installation
   456в†’Using tags for package selection enables:
   457в†’- Customized installations for different machine purposes (work vs. personal vs. development)
   458в†’- Easy addition of new package categories
   459в†’- Minimal setup reduces installation time and disk usage
   460в†’- Clear categorization of package purposes
   461в†’
   462в†’### Machine-Specific Brewfiles
   463в†’Separate Brewfiles per machine allow:
   464в†’- Machine-specific tools without cluttering the main package list
   465в†’- User confirmation before installing potentially large or unwanted packages
   466в†’- Easy experimentation with new tools on one machine
   467в†’- Brewfile symlink enables manual `brew bundle` commands
   468в†’
   469в†’### Static Data File
   470в†’Making the package data file (`packages.yaml`) static ensures:
   471в†’- Available before template engine runs
   472в†’- Simple YAML editing without template syntax
   473в†’- Clear separation between data and logic
   474в†’- Easier for tools to parse and validate
   475в†’- Single source of truth for all package definitions
   476в†’
   477в†’### Script Execution Order Design
   478в†’The numbered script execution order (20, 23, 24, 25, 26, 30) uses 10-point range grouping:
   479в†’- **Categorical grouping**: Scripts in 20-29 range handle package management, 30-39 handle environment managers
   480в†’- **Flexible spacing**: Within each range, scripts use 1-5 point spacing as needed (20, 23, 24, 25, 26)
   481в†’- **Future expansion**: Gaps within ranges allow inserting new scripts without renumbering
   482в†’- **Dependency management**: Package managers install before their packages
   483в†’- **Predictable order**: Clear, documented sequence for troubleshooting
   484в†’
   485в†’**Note on UV timing**: Position 25 (UV tools) runs before position 30 (UV installation). This implies UV must be available through another mechanism (e.g., installed via Homebrew in packages.yaml).
   486в†’
   487в†’## Tag Reference
   488в†’
   489в†’### Tag Usage Across Package Managers
   490в†’
   491в†’This table shows which tags control package installation across all three package management systems:
   492в†’
   493в†’| Tag | Homebrew | UV Tools | SDKMAN | Description |
   494в†’|-----|----------|----------|--------|-------------|
   495в†’| `core` | вњ… Always | вњ… Always | вќЊ | Essential packages for basic system operation |
   496в†’| `dev` | вњ… Optional | вњ… Optional | вњ… **Required** | Development tools, IDEs, language toolchains |
   497в†’| `ai` | вњ… Optional | вњ… Optional | вќЊ | AI/ML tools (Claude, Ollama, LM Studio) |
   498в†’| `work` | вњ… Optional | вњ… Optional | вќЊ | Enterprise/work tools (Teams, Workspaces) |
   499в†’| `personal` | вњ… Optional | вњ… Optional | вќЊ | Personal productivity |
   500в†’| `datascience` | вњ… Optional | вњ… Optional | вќЊ | Data analysis tools (R, RStudio, csvkit) |
   501в†’| `mobile` | вњ… Optional | вњ… Optional | вќЊ | VPN and network tools (Tunnelblick, WireGuard) |
   502в†’
   503в†’### Tag Selection Behavior
   504в†’
   505в†’#### Core Tag (Always Active)
   506в†’- **Homebrew**: Core packages always installed
   507в†’- **UV Tools**: Core tools always installed
   508в†’- **SDKMAN**: Not applicable (requires `dev` tag)
   509в†’
   510в†’#### Development Tag (dev)
   511в†’- **Homebrew**: Installs development packages (Docker, IDEs, etc.)
   512в†’- **UV Tools**: Installs development tools
   513в†’- **SDKMAN**: **Required** - SDKMAN itself and all SDKs only install with `dev` tag
   514в†’
   515в†’#### Other Tags (ai, work, personal, datascience, mobile)
   516в†’- **Homebrew**: Each tag installs corresponding category packages
   517в†’- **UV Tools**: Each tag installs corresponding category tools
   518в†’- **SDKMAN**: Not used (only `dev` category exists)
   519в†’
   520в†’### Data File Structure
   521в†’
   522в†’#### packages.yaml (Unified Package Definition)
   523в†’```yaml
   524в†’packages:
   525в†’  darwin:
   526в†’    taps:
   527в†’    - 'isen-ng/dotnet-sdk-versions'
   528в†’    - 'buo/cask-upgrade'
   529в†’
   530в†’    core:
   531в†’      taps: [...]
   532в†’      brews: [...]
   533в†’      casks: [...]
   534в†’      mas: [...]
   535в†’
   536в†’    dev:
   537в†’      taps: [...]
   538в†’      brews: [...]
   539в†’      casks: [...]
   540в†’      mas: [...]
   541в†’      sdkman:       # SDKMAN SDKs (only in dev tag)
   542в†’      - "java 25-tem"
   543в†’      - "java 8.0.462-zulu"
   544в†’      - "liquibase"
   545в†’
   546в†’    ai:
   547в†’      taps: [...]
   548в†’      brews: [...]
   549в†’      casks: [...]
   550в†’      uv:           # UV Python tools (can be in any tag)
   551в†’      - "basic-memory"
   552в†’      - "claude-monitor"
   553в†’      - "git+https://github.com/org/repo@latest"
   554в†’
   555в†’    # ... other tags (work, personal, datascience, mobile)
   556в†’```
   557в†’
   558в†’### Installation Scripts
   559в†’
   560в†’| Script | Position | Frequency | Purpose |
   561в†’|--------|----------|-----------|---------|
   562в†’| `run_once_before_darwin-20-install-sdkman.sh.tmpl` | 20 | Once | Install SDKMAN (requires `dev` tag) |
   563в†’| `run_onchange_before_darwin-23-install-packages.sh.tmpl` | 23 | On change | Install Homebrew packages from packages.yaml |
   564в†’| `run_onchange_before_darwin-24-install-sdks.sh.tmpl` | 24 | On change | Install SDKs via SDKMAN from packages.yaml (requires `dev` tag) |
   565в†’| `run_onchange_before_darwin-25-install-tools.sh.tmpl` | 25 | On change | Install UV tools from packages.yaml |
   566в†’| `run_onchange_before_darwin-26-brew-bundle-install.sh.tmpl` | 26 | On change | Install machine-specific Homebrew packages |
   567в†’| `run_once_before_darwin-30-install-uv.sh.tmpl` | 30 | Once | Install UV package manager |
   568в†’
```
